text:
Market today: The mood is cautious, with S&P 500 sell signals flashing and options traders bracing for wild swings in major tech. The real driver for your book is the new wave of Trump tariffs, which pose a significant headwind for global tech and chip supply chains.

Your holdings: SOXL is a major concern, down -38.11% as a leveraged chip ETF with a default stop needing attention. SPAL, a brand-new, very volatile leveraged IPO, is down a staggering -52.96% and demands immediate risk management. QQQ is also flagged for a trim, down -3.18%.

Watch: Keep a close eye on how the market reacts to the tariff news, especially its impact on chip and tech giants. Also, monitor the broader market volatility indicated by the S&P 500 sell signals.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing risk in your leveraged positions and respecting all stops, given the market's shaky outlook and tariff uncertainty.
plays: Re-evaluate SOXL's default stop at 200; its deep loss and leveraged nature demand close attention.
Monitor SPAL closely; its new, leveraged status and significant loss mean its 21.38 stop is critical to respect.
Respect QQQ's 670 stop as it's flagged for a trim, especially with broader market weakness.
